title: Shri Naimishnath Vishnu Temple

overview.short: Shri Naimishnath Vishnu Temple, also known as the Naimishnath Devaraja temple or Naimishnarayan Temple or Ramanuj Kot Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Vishnu and Goddess
overview.description: Shri Naimishnath Vishnu Temple, also known as the Naimishnath Devaraja temple or Naimishnarayan Temple or Ramanuj Kot Temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Lord Vishnu and Goddess Lakshmi located in Uttar Pradesh in Naimisharanya town, Sitapur district. It is one of the Divya Desams, the 108 temples of Vishnu revered in Nalayira Divya Prabandham by the 12 poet saints called the Alvars, specifically Thirumangai Alvar. Technically, the whole city is considered a Divya Desam, not just the temple. The temple is believed to be of significant antiquity with contributions at different times from vari

topPlaces.name: Yarpur, Sitapur
topPlaces.description: Yarpur is a village in Misrikh tehsil, Sitapur district, Uttar Pradesh, India. The population was 519 at the 2011 Indian census.

topPlaces.name: Machhrehta
topPlaces.description: Machhrehta is a corresponding community development block of Misrikh tehsil in Sitapur district, Uttar Pradesh, India headed by Praveen Singh (Block Pramukh). The block is situated near Naimisharanya, a significant spiritual and pilgrimage site in India.

topPlaces.name: Misrikh
topPlaces.description: Misrikh is a city and a municipal board in Sitapur district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.

topPlaces.name: Bihat Biram
topPlaces.description: Bihat Biram, also spelled Behat, is a village in Machhrehta block of Sitapur district, Uttar Pradesh, India. As of 2011, its population was 2,852, in 564 households.

items.note: Yarpur is a village in Misrikh tehsil, Sitapur district, Uttar Pradesh, India. The population was 519 at the 2011 Indian census.

Machhrehta is a corresponding community development block of Misrikh tehsil in Sitapur district, Uttar Pradesh, India headed by Praveen Singh (Block Pramukh). The block is situated near Naimisharanya, a significant spiritual and pilgrimage site in India.
Misrikh is a city and a municipal board in Sitapur district in the Indian state of Uttar Pradesh.
Bihat Biram, also spelled Behat, is a village in Machhrehta block of Sitapur district, Uttar Pradesh, India. As of 2011, its population was 2,852, in 564 households.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Shri Naimishnath Vishnu Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Shri Naimishnath Vishnu Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Shri Naimishnath Vishnu Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Chaudhary Charan Singh International Airport, Lucknow (81 km) and the nearest railway station is Lucknow Charbagh (75 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Shri Naimishnath Vishnu Temple?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Shri Naimishnath Vishnu Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Shri Naimishnath Vishnu Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Shri Naimishnath Vishnu Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Shri Naimishnath Vishnu Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Yarpur, Sitapur, Machhrehta, Misrikh, Bihat Biram.
